## GE Vernova Stock Falls Despite Surging AI-Driven Power Orders as Wind Segment Weighs

GEV's order print is live evidence that AI power demand is translating into real order flow — a thesis confirmation for data center infrastructure buildout — but persistent wind segment drag is a segment-mix risk that could cap multiple expansion if it continues.

- GEV shares declined despite headlines showing surging orders tied to AI-driven power demand.
- The wind segment is explicitly cited as a drag offsetting strong power order performance.
- The AI power demand thesis is confirmed by the order flow signal in the headlines.
- High classifier confidence; result is directly material to the datacenter buildout pacing theme.
